sections.paragraph: m. (from the root עָשַׁר), rich, Pro 10:15 14:20 18:11 , and frequently.-(a) in a good sense, honourable, noble, Ecc 10:6 but-(b) in a bad sense, haughty, impious, inasmuch as riches are the fountain of pride, and pride is used in Hebrew as equivalent to impiety, Isa 53:9, compare Job 27:19, and verse 13 Job 27:13. See also הֹולְלִים (under the word הָלַל No. 3), עָנָו and עָנִי, and interpreters on Isa 2:7 53:9 Mat 19:23.
